The Hello* code examples have typically used a very small amount of artificial data and printed out extensive information to show exactly how the algorithms work. The hotgym examples are intended more to show an end-to-end implementation for a real data set and only print out limited information (like the error score every 100 records). So they really serve two different audiences: Hello* for those learning how the algorithms work, and hotgym for those looking for an example they can copy for applying the algorithms to real data.

```
transactions_and_witnesses = (
    (transaction_0, transaction_witness_0),
    (transaction_1, transaction_witness_1),
    (transaction_2, transaction_witness_2),
    ...
)
```

This approach builds in some minimal validation since you can then loop over them like this:

```python
for index, (transaction, transaction_witness) in enumerate(transactions_and_witnesses):
```
